meaning: n.[医]麻疹,风疹,包虫病,痧子
examples: Adults are often immune to German measles.
Rubella is just another name for German measles.
About 93 percent of U.S. residents are immune to measles either because they were vaccinated or they had the disease as a child.
In theory measles could be eradicated - like smallpox it has no animal host.
Measles is particularly dangerous in young infants but anyone can get the disease at any time.
